versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 6 He constructed a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide. 7 He made two cherubim of hammered gold at the ends of the mercy seat, 8 one cherub on one end and one on the other, all made from one piece of gold. 9 And the cherubim had wings that spread upward, overshadowing the mercy seat. The cherubim faced each other, looking toward the mercy seat.

context_lines: In **Exodus 37**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Exodus.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Mercy Seat** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Exodus 37** remains the first control on meaning.
